text: The weird thing about AI progress is that the flashy demo is usually the least important part. The boring loop is where the leverage sits.
topic: why boring loops matter more than flashy demos in AI progress

text: Most teams say they want speed. What they actually want is motion without the emotional cost of committing to a direction.
topic: the difference between speed and motion in teams

text: If a process only works when a senior person is watching, you don't have a process yet. You have theater.
topic: why fragile processes are really theater

text: Small evals are underrated. Not because they are perfect, but because they force you to cash out your taste into something testable.
topic: why small evals force testable taste

text: A lot of 'strategy' is just choosing which problems you are willing to look stupid on for six months.
topic: strategy as choosing which problems to look stupid on

text: The longer I work on systems, the less I trust explanations that sound smooth on first read. Good systems usually feel slightly awkward before they feel obvious.
topic: why good systems feel awkward before they feel obvious

text: The useful question isn't 'can the model do it?' It's 'can the loop keep doing it after the novelty wears off?'
topic: evaluating whether a model loop remains useful after novelty fades

text: You can get surprisingly far by shrinking the problem until feedback gets cheap.
topic: making feedback cheap by shrinking the problem

text: Quick note on the retrieval work: the latest pass is finally stable enough that I trust the trend, but not yet stable enough that I'd call the numbers final. I want one more cleanup pass on the labels before we present anything broadly.
topic: retrieval work is stable enough to trust trends but not final yet

text: Sharing a rough instinct here: I think we're still mixing two different goals in the planning doc. One is shipping something usable next month, the other is proving the long-term architecture. Both are valid, but the current draft keeps pretending they're the same thing.
topic: the planning doc mixes near-term shipping and long-term architecture goals

text: I don't think this needs a bigger rewrite yet. I'd rather make the evaluation loop less annoying first and then see which parts still feel painful after a week of actual use.
topic: improving the evaluation loop before doing a bigger rewrite

text: For tomorrow, my preference is a short working session over a presentation. We already know the broad shape of the problem. What we need now is faster contact with the edge cases.
topic: preferring a short working session over a presentation tomorrow

text: Tentative call: let's keep the scope narrow for v1. The broader concept is interesting, but I don't want us paying the complexity cost before we know where the value is.
topic: keeping v1 narrow to avoid premature complexity

text: Status update: the core path is working, but the failure modes are still too opaque. I'm less worried about raw capability than I am about debugging cost.
topic: status update on a working core path with opaque failure modes
